Fact: Erlotinib/gefitinib lose efficacy against EGFR T790M
Citation: Kobayashi et al. New England Journal of Medicine (2005)
Evidence_summary: First report identifying the EGFR T790M second-site mutation in a gefitinib-relapsed NSCLC patient, with in vitro confirmation that T790M confers gefitinib resistance.

Fact: Erlotinib/gefitinib lose efficacy against EGFR T790M
Evidence_summary: Independently found the T790M second-site mutation in gefitinib/erlotinib-resistant lung adenocarcinomas and showed it blocks drug inhibition of EGFR.
Described AZD9291/osimertinib as an irreversible mutant-selective EGFR-TKI potently inhibiting T790M-mutant lines and xenografts while sparing wild-type EGFR.

Citation: Jänne et al. New England Journal of Medicine (2015)
Evidence_summary: First-in-human trial showing AZD9291 produced high, durable response rates specifically in T790M-positive EGFR-TKI-resistant NSCLC.

Fact: Sotorasib & adagrasib covalently target KRAS G12C at Cys12
Evidence_summary: Original proof-of-concept that small molecules covalently bind the mutant cysteine of KRAS(G12C); crystal structures defined engagement of the switch-II pocket.

Fact: Sotorasib & adagrasib covalently target KRAS G12C at Cys12
Evidence_summary: Characterized AMG 510 (sotorasib) as a covalent KRAS(G12C) inhibitor locking the protein inactive via Cys12, with structural and in vivo antitumor data.

Fact: Sotorasib & adagrasib covalently target KRAS G12C at Cys12
Evidence_summary: Characterized MRTX849 (adagrasib) as a covalent, selective KRAS(G12C) inhibitor targeting Cys12, with efficacy across G12C models and initial patient responses.

Fact: Sotorasib & adagrasib covalently target KRAS G12C at Cys12
Citation: Fell et al. Journal of Medicinal Chemistry (2020)
Evidence_summary: Reported discovery of clinical candidate MRTX849 (adagrasib), including its covalent Cys12 binding mode and crystallographic characterization.
